ground_truth.title: Early ALS symptoms?
ground_truth.author: lulug30
ground_truth.main_content:
lulug30
Apr 12, 2024

Hi and thank you to anyone who takes the time to reply to me.

I'm just looking for a bit of reassurance please.

In June 2023, I visited a neurologist as I was experiencing a tingling/buzzing sensation in my hands and feet. I also experienced some random muscle twitches all over my body. MRI head came back clear and I was diagnosed with Functional Neurological Disorder (FND) by the neurologist.
After a few weeks, my symptoms went away. She thought the FND could have started as a result of stress or long covid.

3 weeks ago my right upper eyelid started twitching - it only happens when I blink hard or when I sneeze/flinch my eyes tight. This is still happening.
In addition to this, I have body wide twitching - I'm feeling it in my arms, legs, face, calves and abdomen.
Does this sound like ALS early symptoms? Or could it just be related to FND flaring up again. My mind is whirring!

Thank you to anyone who takes the time
To answer me

ShiftKicker
Apr 12, 2024

Hello-

Have a read here: Read Before Posting. It covers a fair number of things that bring people to this forum. The biggest point is that twitching absent clinical weakness and sensory issues that come and go point in a different direction than ALS/MND. Frustrating for you to maybe not have answers, but excellent news about it not being MND.

If you're interested in the effects of covid/long covid, we have compiled some links to info here: Covid19 and Neurological Symptoms. There are also some excellent discussion and support groups on FB and reddit where you can talk with others who are experiencing similar.

Take care

lulug30
Apr 12, 2024

Hi,

Thank you for replying.

No, I don't have any weakness anywhere. Just the eyelid twitch (which happens when I blink hard) and twitches all over the body which are involuntary.

Thank you for the covid 19 and neurological symptom links. I will take a look through as all these symptoms seem to have started after having Covid.

Many thanks again

lgelb
Apr 13, 2024

An eyelid twitch that you can cause by irritating the muscle is often related to lack of sleep, dry eyes, and/or dehydration. Best advice is not to irritate it.

Bodywide twitches while still being able to do normal things are essentially the opposite of ALS.

Covid infects potentially any organ since it infects the blood vessels, that carry nutrition into the cells all over the body. But if Covid is the culprit and residual twitching is what you're seeing, that's a way better outcome than much of long Covid. Get some sleep, drink a fruit smoothie, live your life.

lulug30
Apr 22, 2024

Thanks for your reply.
Still experiencing the eye twitch and body wide twitches. The hot spots seem to change...last week most of the twitches were in my abdomen, and this week it's centred by my armpit/chest area.

The last time I had confirmed covid and these symptoms was last April 2023. Surprised to have them all flare up again.

ShiftKicker
Apr 22, 2024

You might benefit from joining a group that is dedicated to the effects of covid. There is research showing that people experience long term issues, even if the initial infection was mild and/or they experienced an asymptomatic infection. Reddit and Facebook are both sites that have multiple groups for support and discussion.

Take care.
